info.description: This is a sample authentication and authorization API built with Go and Gin.
info.title: Authentication and Authorization API

post.description: Register a secondary email address for 2FA recovery (sends verification email)

delete.description: Remove the backup email address from the user account

post.description: Disable backup-email-based 2FA for the authenticated user. No verification code required — authentication alone is sufficient.
Enable backup-email-based 2FA for the authenticated user. The backup email must already be verified.
Resend a new 2FA verification code to the user's backup email during login

get.description: Return the current backup email address and whether it is verified
Confirm a backup email using the token from the verification email

post.description: Disable 2FA for the user (requires TOTP code or email 2FA code)
Enable email-based 2FA for the user (sends codes to registered email)
Resend a new 2FA verification code to the user's email during login
Enable 2FA for the user after successful verification
Generate a 2FA secret and QR code for user setup
Verify the 2FA code during the login process (supports TOTP, email code, or recovery code)

get.description: Get the 2FA methods available for the current application

post.description: Start the WebAuthn assertion ceremony for 2FA verification during login
Complete the WebAuthn assertion ceremony for 2FA verification during login
Generate and display new recovery codes (requires password and/or TOTP code)
Enable SMS-based 2FA for the user (phone number must be verified first)
Resend a new SMS 2FA verification code to the user's phone during login

get.description: List all trusted devices for the authenticated user in the current app

delete.description: Revoke all trusted devices for the authenticated user in the current app
Revoke a specific trusted device by ID

post.description: Verify the initial 2FA setup with a TOTP code

get.description: Retrieve the authenticated user's activity logs with pagination and filtering
Retrieve list of available activity log event types for filtering
Export the authenticated user's activity logs as CSV or JSON (max 10,000 rows). Use the X-Export-Truncated response header to detect if the result was capped.

parameters.description: Export format: csv or json (default: json)
parameters.name: format

get.description: Retrieve a specific activity log by ID (users can only access their own logs)
Retrieve all users' activity logs with pagination and filtering (admin access required)
Export all users' activity logs as CSV or JSON (max 10,000 rows). Use the X-Export-Truncated response header to detect if the result was capped.

parameters.description: Export format: csv or json (default: json)
parameters.name: format

post.description: Register a new application under a specific tenant

get.description: Get paginated delivery history for all webhook endpoints of an application
get.summary: List all delivery logs for an app (admin)

get.description: Returns all webhook endpoints registered for the given application
get.summary: List webhook endpoints for an app (admin)

post.description: Register a new webhook endpoint for an application. Returns the secret only once.

get.description: Retrieve details of a specific application including OAuth configurations
Retrieve the default SMTP server configuration for an application
get.summary: Get default SMTP config for application

put.description: Create or update an SMTP server configuration for an application

delete.description: Remove all SMTP server configurations for an application (falls back to global)

get.description: Retrieve all SMTP server configurations for a specific application

post.description: Send a test email to verify the default SMTP configuration for an application

get.description: Retrieve all IP access rules (allow/block) for a specific application
get.summary: List IP rules for an application

post.description: Add a new IP access rule (allow or block) for a specific application
Evaluate whether a specific IP address is allowed to access an application based on its IP rules

get.description: Retrieve a specific IP access rule by its ID

put.description: Update an existing IP access rule by its ID

delete.description: Remove an IP access rule by its ID

post.description: Configure OAuth provider credentials (Google, GitHub, etc.) for an application
Send an email of the specified type using app's SMTP config and templates

get.description: Retrieve all SMTP server configurations across all applications

post.description: Create a new SMTP server configuration for an application

get.description: Retrieve a specific SMTP server configuration by its ID

put.description: Update an existing SMTP server configuration by its ID

delete.description: Remove a specific SMTP server configuration by its ID

post.description: Send a test email to verify a specific SMTP configuration
post.summary: Send test email by config ID

get.description: Retrieve email templates for a specific app or global defaults

post.description: Create or update an email template for a specific app or as global default
Render a template with sample variables for preview

get.description: Retrieve a specific email template by ID

delete.description: Remove an email template by ID

get.description: Retrieve all registered email types

post.description: Register a new custom email type for use in templates

get.description: Retrieve a specific email type by its code

put.description: Update an existing email type's name, description, subject, or variables

delete.description: Delete a custom email type (system types cannot be deleted)

get.description: Returns all variables that the system can automatically resolve from user profiles, app settings, or that must be passed explicitly. Use this as a reference when adding variables to email types.

get.summary: List OIDC clients for an app

get.description: Retrieve all available permissions in the system

post.description: Create a new custom permission (resource:action pair)

get.description: Retrieve all roles (including permissions) for a specific application

post.description: Create a custom role for an application

get.description: Retrieve a specific role with its permissions

put.description: Update a role's name and description (system roles cannot be renamed)

delete.description: Delete a custom role (system roles cannot be deleted)

put.description: Replace all permissions for a role with the specified permission IDs

get.description: Retrieve all user-role assignments for a specific application with pagination

post.description: Assign a role to a user within an application
post.summary: Assign a role to a user

delete.description: Remove a role assignment from a user within an application
delete.summary: Revoke a role from a user

get.description: Retrieve all roles assigned to a specific user in an application
Retrieve a paginated list of all tenants

post.description: Register a new tenant organization in the system

get.description:
Export all users as CSV or JSON (max 10,000 rows). Optionally filter by app_id or search term.
Use the X-Export-Truncated response header to detect if the result was capped at 10,000 rows.
get.summary: Export users as CSV or JSON (Admin)

parameters.description: Export format: csv or json (default: csv)
parameters.name: format

post.description:
Upload a CSV or JSON file to bulk-create users under a specific application.
The app_id query parameter is required. Duplicate emails are skipped and reported.
Imported users have no password — they must use the password reset flow to set one.
CSV expected columns: email (required), name, first_name, last_name, locale (all optional).
JSON: top-level array or {"users":[...]} object, same fields.
post.summary: Bulk import users from CSV or JSON (Admin)

parameters.description: CSV or JSON file to import
parameters.name: file

get.description: Returns all trusted devices registered by a specific user across all apps
get.summary: List trusted devices for a user

delete.description: Removes all trusted devices for a user, forcing full 2FA on all devices
delete.summary: Revoke all trusted devices for a user

delete.description: Removes a specific trusted device, forcing the user to re-authenticate with 2FA on that device

get.description: Returns all registered webhook endpoints across all applications
Get paginated delivery history for a specific webhook endpoint

put.description: Enable or disable a webhook endpoint

get.description: Returns enabled social providers and OIDC availability for the login/register UI
get.summary: Get public login configuration for an app

get.description: Returns all webhook endpoints for this application

post.description: Register a new webhook endpoint for this application. Returns the secret only once.

get.description: Handles Facebook OAuth2 callback and returns JWT tokens
Initiates OAuth flow to link a Facebook account to the authenticated user
Handles callback from Facebook OAuth to link account to existing user
Redirects user to Facebook OAuth2 login page
Handles GitHub OAuth2 callback and returns JWT tokens
Initiates OAuth flow to link a GitHub account to the authenticated user
Handles callback from GitHub OAuth to link account to existing user
Redirects user to GitHub OAuth2 login page
Handles Google OAuth2 callback and returns JWT tokens
Initiates OAuth flow to link a Google account to the authenticated user
Handles callback from Google OAuth to link account to existing user
Redirects user to Google OAuth2 login page

post.description: Confirms merging a social-login provider into an existing email/password account.

get.description: Validates a JWT token and returns basic user info for external services

post.description: Initiate password reset process

get.description: Checks the connectivity and latency of PostgreSQL, Redis, and SMTP. Returns 200 when all configured components are up, 503 when any are down.

post.description: Authenticate user and issue JWTs
Logout user and revoke refresh token
Send a magic link to the user's email for passwordless authentication. Always returns 200 regardless of whether the email exists (to prevent enumeration).
post.summary: Request a magic link login email

post.description: Verify the magic link token from the email and return access/refresh tokens. The token is single-use and expires after 10 minutes.
post.summary: Verify a magic link token and log in

get.description: Exposes application and runtime metrics in Prometheus exposition format. Requires Admin API Key authentication.

parameters.description: URI to redirect to after logout
parameters.name: post_logout_redirect_uri

post.description: Start a passwordless login ceremony using discoverable credentials (passkeys)
Complete the passwordless login ceremony with the client's assertion response
Start the WebAuthn registration ceremony to add a new passkey
Complete the WebAuthn registration ceremony with the client's attestation response

get.description: List all registered passkeys for the authenticated user

put.description: Update the friendly name of a registered passkey

post.description: Register a phone number for SMS 2FA (sends verification SMS)

delete.description: Remove the phone number from the user account

get.description: Return the current phone number and whether it is verified

post.description: Confirm a phone number using the code from the verification SMS

get.description: Retrieve authenticated user's profile information

put.description: Update authenticated user's profile information (name, first_name, last_name, profile_picture, locale)

delete.description: Delete authenticated user's account permanently (requires password verification and confirmation)

put.description: Update authenticated user's email address (requires password verification and email re-verification)
Update authenticated user's password (requires current password verification)

post.description: Set a password for a user who registered via social login and has no password yet. Returns 409 if a password is already set.
post.summary: Set initial password for social-only users

get.description: Returns all social accounts linked to the authenticated user

delete.description: Removes a linked social account from the authenticated user's profile

post.description: Get new access token using refresh token
Register a new user with email and password
Resend verification email to user. Returns a generic success message regardless of whether the email exists or is already verified (to prevent email enumeration).
Complete password reset process

get.description: List all active sessions (devices/IPs) for the authenticated user

delete.description: Revoke all sessions except the current one
Revoke a specific session by its ID (logout a specific device)

get.description: Verify user's email address

locked_until.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the lockout expires

login_display_name.description: Display name shown on login page

oidc_client_login_theme.description:
OIDCClientLoginTheme is the login_theme of the first active OIDC client for this app.
"app" means the client follows the app's own theme (frontend should send ?ui_theme=).
Empty string means no active OIDC client exists.

pw_min_length.description: Password Policy — exposed so the frontend can show real-time requirements before submission

pw_require_lower.description: Require at least one lowercase letter

pw_require_symbol.description: Require at least one special character

pw_require_upper.description: Require at least one uppercase letter

sms_2fa_enabled.description: whether SMS is available as a 2FA method

two_fa_enabled.description: whether 2FA is allowed for this app

two_fa_required.description: whether every user must set up 2FA before accessing the app

reset_password_path.description: Email Action Link Paths (empty = system defaults apply)

pending_email.description: set when a verification is in progress

retry_after.description: Advisory: seconds the client should wait before retrying

site_key.description: reCAPTCHA site key for the client to render the widget

latency_ms.description: Round-trip time in milliseconds (0 when unconfigured)

reset_password_path.description: Email Action Link Paths (optional; empty = use system defaults)

login_primary_color.description: LoginPrimaryColor overrides Bootstrap's default primary color (e.g. "#4f46e5"). Empty = Bootstrap default.

login_theme.description:
LoginTheme controls the color scheme of OIDC pages: "app" (inherit from Application),
"auto" (default, follow OS preference), "light", or "dark".

password.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

smtp_password.description: #nosec G101 -- This is a DTO field

captcha_token.description: Google reCAPTCHA response token (required when CAPTCHA is triggered)

password.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

access_token.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

password_expired.description: true when the password has expired; no tokens are issued in this case

refresh_token.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

access_token.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

refresh_token.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

app_id.description: optional: overrides the X-App-ID header for multi-app token disambiguation

token.description: #nosec G101 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

merge_token.description: #nosec G101 -- DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

password.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

refresh_token.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

password.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

token.description: #nosec G101 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

device_name.description: Human-readable label for the trusted device

remember_device.description: When true, create a trusted device record

method.description: "totp" or "email" - indicates which 2FA method the user has configured

password.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

login_primary_color.description: LoginPrimaryColor overrides Bootstrap's default primary color. Empty = Bootstrap default.

login_theme.description:
LoginTheme controls the color scheme of OIDC pages: "app" (inherit from Application),
"auto", "light", or "dark".

client_secret.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a DTO field, not a hardcoded credential

secret.description: #nosec G101,G117 -- This is a response field for TOTP secret, not a hardcoded credential

AdminApiKey.description: Admin API Key for protected admin routes
AdminApiKey.name: X-Admin-API-Key

AppApiKey.description: Per-application API Key for app-scoped routes
AppApiKey.name: X-App-API-Key

AppID.description: The UUID of the application context
AppID.name: X-App-ID
